{"key_event":"Security-state expansion amid sanctions and protests","reasoning":"Civil society retained and slightly expanded mobilizing capacity through smartphones, women-led activism, and labor/pensioner protests, but sanctions, arrests, party/NGO suppression, and the post-2009 crackdown kept organization fragile. The state’s fiscal base was hurt by sanctions, yet IRGC expansion, cyber-surveillance, regional militarization, and the 2019 shutdown/crackdown preserved very high coercive reach.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":4.8,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.8}
